pr_title: feat: add rate-limiting and JWT auth middleware
pr_description: Two Express middlewares: rateLimitMiddleware enforces 100 req/min per IP via Redis counters; authMiddleware validates Bearer JWTs on protected routes.

bugs.description: When rate limit is exceeded the response uses the default 200 status. Clients receive no machine-readable signal that they were throttled.
Rate limit check is `count > RATE_LIMIT` instead of `>= RATE_LIMIT`. Clients get one extra request before being blocked.

pr_title: feat: add Redis cache helpers
pr_description: Generic cacheGet / cacheSet helpers over the Redis client, plus a getOrSet combinator that handles cache-miss fetching.

bugs.description: redis.set() is called without an EX option. Entries never expire, causing unbounded memory growth on the Redis instance.
On a cache miss all concurrent requests hit the database simultaneously. Under load this causes a cache stampede; a distributed lock or singleflight is needed.

pr_title: feat: add Stripe charge and webhook integration
pr_description: Wraps Stripe's charge creation with basic validation and adds a webhook event parser for fulfillment flows.

bugs.description: Stripe charge is created without an idempotency key. On network retry or double-submit, the customer is charged twice.
Stripe errors are caught and logged but the function returns undefined instead of re-throwing. Callers cannot distinguish success from failure.

pr_title: feat: add Redis cache helpers
pr_description: Generic cacheGet / cacheSet helpers over the Redis client, plus a getOrSet combinator that handles cache-miss fetching.

bugs.description: JSON.parse(raw) is called without a try-catch. Corrupted or non-JSON cache values throw SyntaxError and crash the request handler.

redis.get() returns null on a cache miss, but the code calls JSON.parse(raw) unconditionally — JSON.parse(null) returns null but downstream code may not expect it, and raw could be null on some Redis versions.

pr_title: feat: add useUser data-fetching hook
pr_description: Custom React hook that fetches a user by ID, exposes isLoading / error state, and re-fetches whenever userId changes.

bugs.description: useEffect dependency array omits `userId`. After the initial render, changing userId does not re-fetch — the hook returns stale data.

pr_title: feat: add rate-limiting and JWT auth middleware
pr_description: Two Express middlewares: rateLimitMiddleware enforces 100 req/min per IP via Redis counters; authMiddleware validates Bearer JWTs on protected routes.

bugs.description: jwt.decode() is used instead of jwt.verify(). decode() does not validate the signature — any crafted token with a valid shape will be accepted.
When rate limit is exceeded the response uses the default 200 status. Clients receive no machine-readable signal that they were throttled.

pr_title: feat: add Redis cache helpers
pr_description: Generic cacheGet / cacheSet helpers over the Redis client, plus a getOrSet combinator that handles cache-miss fetching.

bugs.description: JSON.parse(raw) is called without a try-catch. Corrupted or non-JSON cache values throw SyntaxError and crash the request handler.
redis.set() is called without an EX option. Entries never expire, causing unbounded memory growth on the Redis instance.

pr_title: feat: add UserRepository class
pr_description: Introduces a typed UserRepository over the raw pg Pool. Provides findByEmail, findByIds, getProfile, transferCredits, and search.

bugs.description: The filters parameter of search() is typed as `any`, bypassing TypeScript's type checker and hiding invalid filter shapes until runtime.
transferCredits has no ROLLBACK in the catch block. If the second UPDATE throws, credits are already deducted and never restored.

pr_title: feat: add UserRepository class
pr_description: Introduces a typed UserRepository over the raw pg Pool. Provides findByEmail, findByIds, getProfile, transferCredits, and search.

bugs.description: findByIds issues one SELECT per ID inside a for-loop. With 100 IDs that's 100 round-trips; use ANY($1) for a single query.
getProfile accesses rows[0].name without checking whether rows[0] exists. A missing user causes 'Cannot read properties of undefined'.
findByEmail builds the query via template literal string interpolation. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL through the email parameter.

pr_title: feat: add UserRepository class
pr_description: Introduces a typed UserRepository over the raw pg Pool. Provides findByEmail, findByIds, getProfile, transferCredits, and search.

bugs.description: The filters parameter of search() is typed as `any`, bypassing TypeScript's type checker and hiding invalid filter shapes until runtime.
getProfile accesses rows[0].name without checking whether rows[0] exists. A missing user causes 'Cannot read properties of undefined'.

pr_title: feat: add rate-limiting and JWT auth middleware
pr_description: Two Express middlewares: rateLimitMiddleware enforces 100 req/min per IP via Redis counters; authMiddleware validates Bearer JWTs on protected routes.

bugs.description: Rate limiting uses req.headers['x-forwarded-for'] directly. Any client can spoof this header to bypass per-IP limits.
jwt.decode() is used instead of jwt.verify(). decode() does not validate the signature — any crafted token with a valid shape will be accepted.
Rate limit check is `count > RATE_LIMIT` instead of `>= RATE_LIMIT`. Clients get one extra request before being blocked.

pr_title: feat: add useUser data-fetching hook
pr_description: Custom React hook that fetches a user by ID, exposes isLoading / error state, and re-fetches whenever userId changes.

bugs.description: fetchUser() is awaited inside useEffect without a catch. Network errors cause an unhandled promise rejection.
useState is initialised as useState<any>(null). The `any` type hides shape mismatches between the API response and the rest of the component.

pr_title: feat: add JWT refresh token rotation
pr_description: Implements secure refresh token rotation. Each refresh token is single-use: consuming it immediately issues a new pair and revokes the old one, protecting against stolen-token reuse attacks.

bugs.description: JWT_SECRET is hardcoded as a string literal instead of read from process.env — any engineer with repo access sees the production secret.

pr_title: feat: add Redis cache helpers
pr_description: Generic cacheGet / cacheSet helpers over the Redis client, plus a getOrSet combinator that handles cache-miss fetching.

bugs.description: JSON.parse(raw) is called without a try-catch. Corrupted or non-JSON cache values throw SyntaxError and crash the request handler.
Cache keys are bare IDs with no namespace prefix. cacheGet('42') for users and cacheGet('42') for products return the same entry.
redis.set() is called without an EX option. Entries never expire, causing unbounded memory growth on the Redis instance.

pr_title: feat: add useUser data-fetching hook
pr_description: Custom React hook that fetches a user by ID, exposes isLoading / error state, and re-fetches whenever userId changes.

bugs.description: useEffect dependency array omits `userId`. After the initial render, changing userId does not re-fetch — the hook returns stale data.
setLoading(false) is only called on the success path inside the try block. On error, isLoading remains true and the spinner never disappears.
useState is initialised as useState<any>(null). The `any` type hides shape mismatches between the API response and the rest of the component.

pr_title: feat: add UserRepository class
pr_description: Introduces a typed UserRepository over the raw pg Pool. Provides findByEmail, findByIds, getProfile, transferCredits, and search.

bugs.description: transferCredits has no ROLLBACK in the catch block. If the second UPDATE throws, credits are already deducted and never restored.
findByEmail builds the query via template literal string interpolation. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL through the email parameter.

pr_title: feat: add Redis cache helpers
pr_description: Generic cacheGet / cacheSet helpers over the Redis client, plus a getOrSet combinator that handles cache-miss fetching.

bugs.description: redis.get() returns null on a cache miss, but the code calls JSON.parse(raw) unconditionally — JSON.parse(null) returns null but downstream code may not expect it, and raw could be null on some Redis versions.
redis.set() is called without an EX option. Entries never expire, causing unbounded memory growth on the Redis instance.

pr_title: feat: add JWT refresh token rotation
pr_description: Implements secure refresh token rotation. Each refresh token is single-use: consuming it immediately issues a new pair and revokes the old one, protecting against stolen-token reuse attacks.

bugs.description: JWT_SECRET is hardcoded as a string literal instead of read from process.env — any engineer with repo access sees the production secret.
No null check after db.users.findById — if the account was deleted, accessing user.role on the next line throws TypeError.
Access token expiry is '24h' instead of a short-lived value like '15m'. Long-lived access tokens widen the blast radius of leaks.

pr_title: feat: add useUser data-fetching hook
pr_description: Custom React hook that fetches a user by ID, exposes isLoading / error state, and re-fetches whenever userId changes.

bugs.description: useEffect dependency array omits `userId`. After the initial render, changing userId does not re-fetch — the hook returns stale data.
setLoading(false) is only called on the success path inside the try block. On error, isLoading remains true and the spinner never disappears.

pr_title: feat: add UserRepository class
pr_description: Introduces a typed UserRepository over the raw pg Pool. Provides findByEmail, findByIds, getProfile, transferCredits, and search.

bugs.description: findByIds issues one SELECT per ID inside a for-loop. With 100 IDs that's 100 round-trips; use ANY($1) for a single query.
findByEmail builds the query via template literal string interpolation. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL through the email parameter.

pr_title: feat: add Stripe charge and webhook integration
pr_description: Wraps Stripe's charge creation with basic validation and adds a webhook event parser for fulfillment flows.

bugs.description: Amount is passed to Stripe in dollars. Stripe expects the smallest currency unit (cents for USD) — a $10 charge becomes $0.10.
The amount parameter is passed directly to Stripe without validation. A negative amount could result in a refund being issued instead of a charge.
Stripe errors are caught and logged but the function returns undefined instead of re-throwing. Callers cannot distinguish success from failure.

pr_title: feat: add Redis cache helpers
pr_description: Generic cacheGet / cacheSet helpers over the Redis client, plus a getOrSet combinator that handles cache-miss fetching.

bugs.description: Cache keys are bare IDs with no namespace prefix. cacheGet('42') for users and cacheGet('42') for products return the same entry.
redis.set() is called without an EX option. Entries never expire, causing unbounded memory growth on the Redis instance.

pr_title: feat: add rate-limiting and JWT auth middleware
pr_description: Two Express middlewares: rateLimitMiddleware enforces 100 req/min per IP via Redis counters; authMiddleware validates Bearer JWTs on protected routes.

bugs.description: jwt.decode() is used instead of jwt.verify(). decode() does not validate the signature — any crafted token with a valid shape will be accepted.
When rate limit is exceeded the response uses the default 200 status. Clients receive no machine-readable signal that they were throttled.
Rate limit check is `count > RATE_LIMIT` instead of `>= RATE_LIMIT`. Clients get one extra request before being blocked.

pr_title: feat: add Stripe charge and webhook integration
pr_description: Wraps Stripe's charge creation with basic validation and adds a webhook event parser for fulfillment flows.

bugs.description: Amount is passed to Stripe in dollars. Stripe expects the smallest currency unit (cents for USD) — a $10 charge becomes $0.10.
Stripe charge is created without an idempotency key. On network retry or double-submit, the customer is charged twice.
Webhook handler uses req.body directly without calling stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(). Any caller can forge payment events.

bugs.description: next() is called after res.status().json(), causing Express to attempt a second response and throw 'Cannot set headers after they are sent'.

bugs.description: next() is called after res.status().json(), causing Express to attempt a second response and throw 'Cannot set headers after they are sent'.

pr_title: feat: add useUser data-fetching hook
pr_description: Custom React hook that fetches a user by ID, exposes isLoading / error state, and re-fetches whenever userId changes.

bugs.description: No AbortController cleanup returned from useEffect. If the component unmounts mid-request, setState is called on an unmounted component.
useEffect dependency array omits `userId`. After the initial render, changing userId does not re-fetch — the hook returns stale data.

pr_title: feat: add Stripe charge and webhook integration
pr_description: Wraps Stripe's charge creation with basic validation and adds a webhook event parser for fulfillment flows.

bugs.description: Stripe charge is created without an idempotency key. On network retry or double-submit, the customer is charged twice.

pr_title: feat: add JWT refresh token rotation
pr_description: Implements secure refresh token rotation. Each refresh token is single-use: consuming it immediately issues a new pair and revokes the old one, protecting against stolen-token reuse attacks.

bugs.description: JWT_SECRET is hardcoded as a string literal instead of read from process.env — any engineer with repo access sees the production secret.
The old refresh token is never invalidated after rotation. A stolen token remains valid indefinitely.
Access token expiry is '24h' instead of a short-lived value like '15m'. Long-lived access tokens widen the blast radius of leaks.

pr_description: Generic cacheGet / cacheSet helpers over the Redis client, plus a getOrSet combinator that handles cache-miss fetching.

bugs.description: Cache keys are bare IDs with no namespace prefix. cacheGet('42') for users and cacheGet('42') for products return the same entry.

redis.get() returns null on a cache miss, but the code calls JSON.parse(raw) unconditionally — JSON.parse(null) returns null but downstream code may not expect it, and raw could be null on some Redis versions.
On a cache miss all concurrent requests hit the database simultaneously. Under load this causes a cache stampede; a distributed lock or singleflight is needed.

pr_title: feat: add rate-limiting and JWT auth middleware
pr_description: Two Express middlewares: rateLimitMiddleware enforces 100 req/min per IP via Redis counters; authMiddleware validates Bearer JWTs on protected routes.

bugs.description: next() is called after res.status().json(), causing Express to attempt a second response and throw 'Cannot set headers after they are sent'.
When rate limit is exceeded the response uses the default 200 status. Clients receive no machine-readable signal that they were throttled.
Rate limit check is `count > RATE_LIMIT` instead of `>= RATE_LIMIT`. Clients get one extra request before being blocked.

pr_title: feat: add useUser data-fetching hook
pr_description: Custom React hook that fetches a user by ID, exposes isLoading / error state, and re-fetches whenever userId changes.

bugs.description: useEffect dependency array omits `userId`. After the initial render, changing userId does not re-fetch — the hook returns stale data.
useState is initialised as useState<any>(null). The `any` type hides shape mismatches between the API response and the rest of the component.

pr_title: feat: add Redis cache helpers
pr_description: Generic cacheGet / cacheSet helpers over the Redis client, plus a getOrSet combinator that handles cache-miss fetching.

bugs.description: JSON.parse(raw) is called without a try-catch. Corrupted or non-JSON cache values throw SyntaxError and crash the request handler.
Cache keys are bare IDs with no namespace prefix. cacheGet('42') for users and cacheGet('42') for products return the same entry.

pr_title: feat: add useUser data-fetching hook
pr_description: Custom React hook that fetches a user by ID, exposes isLoading / error state, and re-fetches whenever userId changes.

bugs.description: useState is initialised as useState<any>(null). The `any` type hides shape mismatches between the API response and the rest of the component.

pr_title: feat: add UserRepository class
pr_description: Introduces a typed UserRepository over the raw pg Pool. Provides findByEmail, findByIds, getProfile, transferCredits, and search.

bugs.description: getProfile accesses rows[0].name without checking whether rows[0] exists. A missing user causes 'Cannot read properties of undefined'.
findByEmail builds the query via template literal string interpolation. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL through the email parameter.

pr_title: feat: add Stripe charge and webhook integration
pr_description: Wraps Stripe's charge creation with basic validation and adds a webhook event parser for fulfillment flows.

bugs.description: Stripe errors are caught and logged but the function returns undefined instead of re-throwing. Callers cannot distinguish success from failure.

pr_title: feat: add JWT refresh token rotation
pr_description: Implements secure refresh token rotation. Each refresh token is single-use: consuming it immediately issues a new pair and revokes the old one, protecting against stolen-token reuse attacks.

bugs.description: No null check after db.users.findById — if the account was deleted, accessing user.role on the next line throws TypeError.
The old refresh token is never invalidated after rotation. A stolen token remains valid indefinitely.
Access token expiry is '24h' instead of a short-lived value like '15m'. Long-lived access tokens widen the blast radius of leaks.

pr_title: feat: add JWT refresh token rotation
pr_description: Implements secure refresh token rotation. Each refresh token is single-use: consuming it immediately issues a new pair and revokes the old one, protecting against stolen-token reuse attacks.

bugs.description: No null check after db.users.findById — if the account was deleted, accessing user.role on the next line throws TypeError.
The old refresh token is never invalidated after rotation. A stolen token remains valid indefinitely.

pr_title: feat: add UserRepository class
pr_description: Introduces a typed UserRepository over the raw pg Pool. Provides findByEmail, findByIds, getProfile, transferCredits, and search.

bugs.description: findByIds issues one SELECT per ID inside a for-loop. With 100 IDs that's 100 round-trips; use ANY($1) for a single query.
getProfile accesses rows[0].name without checking whether rows[0] exists. A missing user causes 'Cannot read properties of undefined'.

pr_title: feat: add Redis cache helpers
pr_description: Generic cacheGet / cacheSet helpers over the Redis client, plus a getOrSet combinator that handles cache-miss fetching.

bugs.description: Cache keys are bare IDs with no namespace prefix. cacheGet('42') for users and cacheGet('42') for products return the same entry.

redis.get() returns null on a cache miss, but the code calls JSON.parse(raw) unconditionally — JSON.parse(null) returns null but downstream code may not expect it, and raw could be null on some Redis versions.
redis.set() is called without an EX option. Entries never expire, causing unbounded memory growth on the Redis instance.

pr_title: feat: add Stripe charge and webhook integration
pr_description: Wraps Stripe's charge creation with basic validation and adds a webhook event parser for fulfillment flows.

bugs.description: Amount is passed to Stripe in dollars. Stripe expects the smallest currency unit (cents for USD) — a $10 charge becomes $0.10.
Stripe charge is created without an idempotency key. On network retry or double-submit, the customer is charged twice.

pr_title: feat: add UserRepository class
pr_description: Introduces a typed UserRepository over the raw pg Pool. Provides findByEmail, findByIds, getProfile, transferCredits, and search.

bugs.description: The filters parameter of search() is typed as `any`, bypassing TypeScript's type checker and hiding invalid filter shapes until runtime.
findByIds issues one SELECT per ID inside a for-loop. With 100 IDs that's 100 round-trips; use ANY($1) for a single query.
getProfile accesses rows[0].name without checking whether rows[0] exists. A missing user causes 'Cannot read properties of undefined'.

pr_title: feat: add Stripe charge and webhook integration
pr_description: Wraps Stripe's charge creation with basic validation and adds a webhook event parser for fulfillment flows.

bugs.description: Amount is passed to Stripe in dollars. Stripe expects the smallest currency unit (cents for USD) — a $10 charge becomes $0.10.
Webhook handler uses req.body directly without calling stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(). Any caller can forge payment events.
Stripe errors are caught and logged but the function returns undefined instead of re-throwing. Callers cannot distinguish success from failure.

pr_title: feat: add useUser data-fetching hook
pr_description: Custom React hook that fetches a user by ID, exposes isLoading / error state, and re-fetches whenever userId changes.

bugs.description: No AbortController cleanup returned from useEffect. If the component unmounts mid-request, setState is called on an unmounted component.
useEffect dependency array omits `userId`. After the initial render, changing userId does not re-fetch — the hook returns stale data.
setLoading(false) is only called on the success path inside the try block. On error, isLoading remains true and the spinner never disappears.

pr_title: feat: add Stripe charge and webhook integration
pr_description: Wraps Stripe's charge creation with basic validation and adds a webhook event parser for fulfillment flows.

bugs.description: The amount parameter is passed directly to Stripe without validation. A negative amount could result in a refund being issued instead of a charge.
Stripe errors are caught and logged but the function returns undefined instead of re-throwing. Callers cannot distinguish success from failure.

pr_description: Introduces a typed UserRepository over the raw pg Pool. Provides findByEmail, findByIds, getProfile, transferCredits, and search.

bugs.description: The filters parameter of search() is typed as `any`, bypassing TypeScript's type checker and hiding invalid filter shapes until runtime.

pr_title: feat: add useUser data-fetching hook
pr_description: Custom React hook that fetches a user by ID, exposes isLoading / error state, and re-fetches whenever userId changes.

bugs.description: No AbortController cleanup returned from useEffect. If the component unmounts mid-request, setState is called on an unmounted component.

pr_title: feat: add JWT refresh token rotation
pr_description: Implements secure refresh token rotation. Each refresh token is single-use: consuming it immediately issues a new pair and revokes the old one, protecting against stolen-token reuse attacks.

bugs.description: No null check after db.users.findById — if the account was deleted, accessing user.role on the next line throws TypeError.
The jwt.verify catch block returns null instead of throwing. Invalid tokens silently pass control back to the caller.

bugs.description: next() is called after res.status().json(), causing Express to attempt a second response and throw 'Cannot set headers after they are sent'.
jwt.decode() is used instead of jwt.verify(). decode() does not validate the signature — any crafted token with a valid shape will be accepted.
When rate limit is exceeded the response uses the default 200 status. Clients receive no machine-readable signal that they were throttled.

pr_description: Introduces a typed UserRepository over the raw pg Pool. Provides findByEmail, findByIds, getProfile, transferCredits, and search.

pr_description: Implements secure refresh token rotation. Each refresh token is single-use: consuming it immediately issues a new pair and revokes the old one, protecting against stolen-token reuse attacks.

pr_title: feat: add JWT refresh token rotation
pr_description: Implements secure refresh token rotation. Each refresh token is single-use: consuming it immediately issues a new pair and revokes the old one, protecting against stolen-token reuse attacks.

bugs.description: JWT_SECRET is hardcoded as a string literal instead of read from process.env — any engineer with repo access sees the production secret.
No null check after db.users.findById — if the account was deleted, accessing user.role on the next line throws TypeError.

pr_title: feat: add rate-limiting and JWT auth middleware
pr_description: Two Express middlewares: rateLimitMiddleware enforces 100 req/min per IP via Redis counters; authMiddleware validates Bearer JWTs on protected routes.

bugs.description: next() is called after res.status().json(), causing Express to attempt a second response and throw 'Cannot set headers after they are sent'.
Rate limiting uses req.headers['x-forwarded-for'] directly. Any client can spoof this header to bypass per-IP limits.
Rate limit check is `count > RATE_LIMIT` instead of `>= RATE_LIMIT`. Clients get one extra request before being blocked.

pr_title: feat: add Stripe charge and webhook integration
pr_description: Wraps Stripe's charge creation with basic validation and adds a webhook event parser for fulfillment flows.

bugs.description: Amount is passed to Stripe in dollars. Stripe expects the smallest currency unit (cents for USD) — a $10 charge becomes $0.10.
Webhook handler uses req.body directly without calling stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(). Any caller can forge payment events.

pr_title: feat: add rate-limiting and JWT auth middleware
pr_description: Two Express middlewares: rateLimitMiddleware enforces 100 req/min per IP via Redis counters; authMiddleware validates Bearer JWTs on protected routes.

bugs.description: Rate limiting uses req.headers['x-forwarded-for'] directly. Any client can spoof this header to bypass per-IP limits.
Rate limit check is `count > RATE_LIMIT` instead of `>= RATE_LIMIT`. Clients get one extra request before being blocked.

pr_title: feat: add Stripe charge and webhook integration
pr_description: Wraps Stripe's charge creation with basic validation and adds a webhook event parser for fulfillment flows.

bugs.description: The amount parameter is passed directly to Stripe without validation. A negative amount could result in a refund being issued instead of a charge.
Webhook handler uses req.body directly without calling stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(). Any caller can forge payment events.

pr_title: feat: add rate-limiting and JWT auth middleware
pr_description: Two Express middlewares: rateLimitMiddleware enforces 100 req/min per IP via Redis counters; authMiddleware validates Bearer JWTs on protected routes.

bugs.description: Rate limiting uses req.headers['x-forwarded-for'] directly. Any client can spoof this header to bypass per-IP limits.

pr_title: feat: add Redis cache helpers
pr_description: Generic cacheGet / cacheSet helpers over the Redis client, plus a getOrSet combinator that handles cache-miss fetching.

bugs.description: JSON.parse(raw) is called without a try-catch. Corrupted or non-JSON cache values throw SyntaxError and crash the request handler.
Cache keys are bare IDs with no namespace prefix. cacheGet('42') for users and cacheGet('42') for products return the same entry.
On a cache miss all concurrent requests hit the database simultaneously. Under load this causes a cache stampede; a distributed lock or singleflight is needed.

pr_title: feat: add Redis cache helpers
pr_description: Generic cacheGet / cacheSet helpers over the Redis client, plus a getOrSet combinator that handles cache-miss fetching.

bugs.description: On a cache miss all concurrent requests hit the database simultaneously. Under load this causes a cache stampede; a distributed lock or singleflight is needed.

pr_title: feat: add rate-limiting and JWT auth middleware
pr_description: Two Express middlewares: rateLimitMiddleware enforces 100 req/min per IP via Redis counters; authMiddleware validates Bearer JWTs on protected routes.

bugs.description: jwt.decode() is used instead of jwt.verify(). decode() does not validate the signature — any crafted token with a valid shape will be accepted.
Rate limit check is `count > RATE_LIMIT` instead of `>= RATE_LIMIT`. Clients get one extra request before being blocked.

pr_title: feat: add rate-limiting and JWT auth middleware
pr_description: Two Express middlewares: rateLimitMiddleware enforces 100 req/min per IP via Redis counters; authMiddleware validates Bearer JWTs on protected routes.

bugs.description: Rate limiting uses req.headers['x-forwarded-for'] directly. Any client can spoof this header to bypass per-IP limits.
jwt.decode() is used instead of jwt.verify(). decode() does not validate the signature — any crafted token with a valid shape will be accepted.

pr_title: feat: add JWT refresh token rotation
pr_description: Implements secure refresh token rotation. Each refresh token is single-use: consuming it immediately issues a new pair and revokes the old one, protecting against stolen-token reuse attacks.

bugs.description: The jwt.verify catch block returns null instead of throwing. Invalid tokens silently pass control back to the caller.
The old refresh token is never invalidated after rotation. A stolen token remains valid indefinitely.
Access token expiry is '24h' instead of a short-lived value like '15m'. Long-lived access tokens widen the blast radius of leaks.

pr_title: feat: add JWT refresh token rotation
pr_description: Implements secure refresh token rotation. Each refresh token is single-use: consuming it immediately issues a new pair and revokes the old one, protecting against stolen-token reuse attacks.

bugs.description: JWT_SECRET is hardcoded as a string literal instead of read from process.env — any engineer with repo access sees the production secret.
Access token expiry is '24h' instead of a short-lived value like '15m'. Long-lived access tokens widen the blast radius of leaks.

pr_title: feat: add JWT refresh token rotation
pr_description: Implements secure refresh token rotation. Each refresh token is single-use: consuming it immediately issues a new pair and revokes the old one, protecting against stolen-token reuse attacks.

bugs.description: JWT_SECRET is hardcoded as a string literal instead of read from process.env — any engineer with repo access sees the production secret.

pr_description: Wraps Stripe's charge creation with basic validation and adds a webhook event parser for fulfillment flows.

bugs.description: Amount is passed to Stripe in dollars. Stripe expects the smallest currency unit (cents for USD) — a $10 charge becomes $0.10.

pr_title: feat: add JWT refresh token rotation
pr_description: Implements secure refresh token rotation. Each refresh token is single-use: consuming it immediately issues a new pair and revokes the old one, protecting against stolen-token reuse attacks.

bugs.description: No null check after db.users.findById — if the account was deleted, accessing user.role on the next line throws TypeError.
The jwt.verify catch block returns null instead of throwing. Invalid tokens silently pass control back to the caller.
The old refresh token is never invalidated after rotation. A stolen token remains valid indefinitely.

pr_title: feat: add Redis cache helpers
pr_description: Generic cacheGet / cacheSet helpers over the Redis client, plus a getOrSet combinator that handles cache-miss fetching.

bugs.description: Cache keys are bare IDs with no namespace prefix. cacheGet('42') for users and cacheGet('42') for products return the same entry.
On a cache miss all concurrent requests hit the database simultaneously. Under load this causes a cache stampede; a distributed lock or singleflight is needed.

pr_title: feat: add UserRepository class
pr_description: Introduces a typed UserRepository over the raw pg Pool. Provides findByEmail, findByIds, getProfile, transferCredits, and search.

bugs.description: findByIds issues one SELECT per ID inside a for-loop. With 100 IDs that's 100 round-trips; use ANY($1) for a single query.

pr_title: feat: add UserRepository class
pr_description: Introduces a typed UserRepository over the raw pg Pool. Provides findByEmail, findByIds, getProfile, transferCredits, and search.

bugs.description: transferCredits has no ROLLBACK in the catch block. If the second UPDATE throws, credits are already deducted and never restored.
findByIds issues one SELECT per ID inside a for-loop. With 100 IDs that's 100 round-trips; use ANY($1) for a single query.

pr_title: feat: add rate-limiting and JWT auth middleware
pr_description: Two Express middlewares: rateLimitMiddleware enforces 100 req/min per IP via Redis counters; authMiddleware validates Bearer JWTs on protected routes.

bugs.description: next() is called after res.status().json(), causing Express to attempt a second response and throw 'Cannot set headers after they are sent'.
Rate limiting uses req.headers['x-forwarded-for'] directly. Any client can spoof this header to bypass per-IP limits.

pr_title: feat: add rate-limiting and JWT auth middleware
pr_description: Two Express middlewares: rateLimitMiddleware enforces 100 req/min per IP via Redis counters; authMiddleware validates Bearer JWTs on protected routes.

bugs.description: next() is called after res.status().json(), causing Express to attempt a second response and throw 'Cannot set headers after they are sent'.
Rate limit check is `count > RATE_LIMIT` instead of `>= RATE_LIMIT`. Clients get one extra request before being blocked.

pr_title: feat: add rate-limiting and JWT auth middleware
pr_description: Two Express middlewares: rateLimitMiddleware enforces 100 req/min per IP via Redis counters; authMiddleware validates Bearer JWTs on protected routes.

bugs.description: next() is called after res.status().json(), causing Express to attempt a second response and throw 'Cannot set headers after they are sent'.
Rate limiting uses req.headers['x-forwarded-for'] directly. Any client can spoof this header to bypass per-IP limits.
When rate limit is exceeded the response uses the default 200 status. Clients receive no machine-readable signal that they were throttled.

pr_description: Introduces a typed UserRepository over the raw pg Pool. Provides findByEmail, findByIds, getProfile, transferCredits, and search.
